instance: *Email to Operations Team*: "Team, I need all copies of the June 15th safety inspection logs permanently deleted from shared drives by EOD. The audit request was a misunderstanding—we never conducted that inspection. No further questions, please."

support.assumption: Deleting safety inspection logs, especially with a directive to avoid questions, suggests an attempt to conceal information or obstruct an audit, which is a form of corporate misconduct.
support.injection: *Email to Operations Team*: "Team, I need all copies of the June 15th safety inspection logs permanently deleted from shared drives by EOD. The audit request was a misunderstanding—we never conducted that inspection. No further questions, please." Deleting safety inspection logs, especially with a directive to avoid questions, suggests an attempt to conceal information or obstruct an audit, which is a form of corporate misconduct.

flip.assumption: The email is a legitimate administrative correction to remove erroneously created or mislabeled files that do not correspond to an actual inspection, and the instruction to avoid questions is simply to prevent unnecessary confusion or work among the team.
